name: TAO Post Expansion Runner v1
edge_mechanism: post-expansion partial-profit runner structure that keeps a core position for trend persistence
thesis: TAO 4h is the best structurally different lane for testing whether post-expansion entries can still work if the trade is explicitly designed to take partial profits quickly while preserving a small core for persistence. The edge is not the expansion bar itself; it is the management structure paired with orderly post-expansion hold conditions.
invalidation_condition: Abort if post-expansion holds are too sparse, if the partial-profit structure cannot offset frequent reversals, or if TAO volatility makes the runner logic unusable.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price expands out of balance, holds above fast value instead of snapping back immediately, pays a first target quickly, and occasionally continues with a residual trend leg.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Transition-heavy chop can still produce short-lived expansions where most expectancy comes from harvesting the first move while still keeping a small runner for the cases that become real trends.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: TAO 4h is appropriate because it expresses expansion and persistence more clearly than ETH while still allowing swing-style management.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: Expansion bars may occur often enough, but if most of them snap back immediately the runner structure will not compensate for churn.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If entries are dense but net expectancy is weak, tighten the hold requirement or de-risk faster before altering the core partial-runner design.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: Frequent first-target hits with a smaller subset of trades delivering the runner payoff.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: TAO 4h plus one cross-lane show positive expectancy from partial-profit-first management with occasional persistence capture.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, too-sparse output, or repeated immediate snapbacks that invalidate the post-expansion hold thesis.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; expansion, hold, and staged exit logic are complementary
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed by using post-expansion hold rather than exact breakout-bar perfection

validation_basket.why_included: Best expressive lane for testing post-expansion staged management.
validation_basket.continue_if: Hold-after-expansion trades are frequent enough and first-target harvesting overcomes reversals.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: Entries are too sparse or snapbacks overwhelm the management structure.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: A reasonable set of partial-profit wins with occasional runner extensions.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the same management-led mechanism survives on another expressive asset.
validation_basket.continue_if: SOL shows similar post-expansion harvesting behavior.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: SOL becomes too whippy for staged exits.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: More frequent but slightly smaller partial-profit opportunities than TAO.

validation_basket.why_included: Tests whether the mechanism transfers to a cleaner major lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH preserves the post-expansion hold-and-run shape.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: The idea only works on high-volatility lanes.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Fewer but cleaner runner opportunities than TAO.
